regimens.setting: First-line, Locally Advanced (Stage IB3, IIA2, IIB, III, IVA)
regimens.evidence_level: Category 1 (for FIGO 2014 stage IIIA, IIIB, IVA); Category 2B (for select FIGO 2018 stage III-IVA)

trial_data.n_patients: 1060 eligible patients with newly diagnosed, previously untreated, high-risk locally advanced cervical cancer
trial_data.pfs: PFS HR estimate 0.59 (95% CI, 0.43–0.82) in exploratory subgroup analysis of 596 patients with FIGO 2014 stage III–IVA disease

regimens.notes: Concurrent platinum-containing chemotherapy with EBRT utilizes cisplatin as a single agent. Pembrolizumab may be added with CRT as follows: cisplatin/RT and pembrolizumab (FIGO 2014 stage IIIA, IIIB, and IVA: category 1); cisplatin/RT and pembrolizumab (select FIGO 2018 stage III–IVA: category 2B). Category 2B is for FIGO 2018 stage IIIC purely on nodal metastasis without concomitant tumor characteristics defined in 2014 FIGO stage IIIA–IIIB. If the FIGO 2018 staging of stage III overlaps with FIGO 2014 IIIA/IIIB staging, category 1 can be applied. [1]

regimens.setting: First-line, Locally Advanced (Stage IB3, IIA2, IIB, III, IVA)
regimens.evidence_level: Category 1 (for FIGO 2014 stage IIIA, IIIB, IVA); Category 2B (for select FIGO 2018 stage III-IVA)

trial_data.n_patients: 1060 eligible patients with newly diagnosed, previously untreated, high-risk locally advanced cervical cancer
trial_data.pfs: PFS HR estimate 0.59 (95% CI, 0.43–0.82) in exploratory subgroup analysis of 596 patients with FIGO 2014 stage III–IVA disease

regimens.notes: For patients who are cisplatin intolerant, carboplatin may be used instead. [1]

regimens.setting: First-line, Locally Advanced (Stage IB3, IIA2, IIB, III, IVA)
regimens.notes: Cisplatin remains the preferred radiosensitizing agent in the primary treatment for patients with locally advanced cervical cancer when used concomitantly with EBRT. [1]

regimens.setting: First-line, Locally Advanced (Stage IB3, IIA2, IIB, III, IVA)

trial_data.n_patients: 500 eligible patients with LACC
trial_data.os: 5-year OS rates were 80% in the induction chemotherapy with chemoradiotherapy group and 72% in the chemoradiotherapy alone group (HR = 0.60; 95% CI, 0.40–0.91; P = .015)
trial_data.pfs: 5-year PFS rates were 72% in the induction chemotherapy with chemoradiotherapy group and 64% in the chemoradiotherapy alone group (HR = 0.65; 95% CI, 0.46–0.91; P = .013)

regimens.notes: Induction chemotherapy (carboplatin/paclitaxel) followed by single-agent cisplatin (or carboplatin) and radiation given according to the INTERLACE protocol could be considered. [6]

regimens.setting: First-line, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 617 patients with previously untreated persistent, recurrent, or metastatic cervical cancer (548 with PD-L1–positive CPS ≥1 tumors)
trial_data.os: At 24 months: 50.4% vs 40.4% (HR, 0.67; 95% CI, 0.54–0.84; P < .001); final updated analysis: median OS 28.6 vs 16.5 months (HR, 0.60; 95% CI, 0.49–0.74; P < .0001)
trial_data.pfs: 10.4 vs 8.2 months (HR, 0.65; 95% CI, 0.53–0.79; P < .001); final updated analysis: median PFS 10.5 vs 8.2 months

regimens.notes: Pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy with or without bevacizumab is a preferred, category 1 treatment option for patients with PD-L1–positive (CPS ≥1) recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer. Checkpoint inhibitors and/or monoclonal antibodies included in this regimen may be continued as maintenance therapy. [7, 8]

regimens.setting: First-line, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 617 patients with previously untreated persistent, recurrent, or metastatic cervical cancer (548 with PD-L1–positive CPS ≥1 tumors)
trial_data.os: At 24 months: 50.4% vs 40.4% (HR, 0.67; 95% CI, 0.54–0.84; P < .001); final updated analysis: median OS 28.6 vs 16.5 months (HR, 0.60; 95% CI, 0.49–0.74; P < .0001)
trial_data.pfs: 10.4 vs 8.2 months (HR, 0.65; 95% CI, 0.53–0.79; P < .001); final updated analysis: median PFS 10.5 vs 8.2 months

regimens.notes: For patients who are cisplatin intolerant, carboplatin may be used instead. [7, 8]

regimens.setting: First-line, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 452 patients with metastatic, persistent, or recurrent cervical cancer
trial_data.os: Median OS 16.8 vs 13.3 months (P = .007) for bevacizumab vs no bevacizumab

regimens.notes: Platinum-based chemotherapy (cisplatin or carboplatin)/paclitaxel with bevacizumab is a preferred, first-line treatment option. [8, 288, 289]

regimens.setting: First-line, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 253 patients with metastatic or recurrent cervical cancer
trial_data.os: Median OS 18.3 months for cisplatin/paclitaxel vs 17.5 months for carboplatin/paclitaxel (HR = 0.994 [90% CI, 0.79–1.25]; P = .032)

regimens.notes: Carboplatin/paclitaxel is noninferior to cisplatin/paclitaxel and may be easier to administer. [292, 303]

regimens.setting: First-line, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 519 patients with measurable, previously untreated metastatic, persistent, or recurrent cervical cancer

regimens.notes: Addition of atezolizumab to bevacizumab plus chemotherapy combination is a category 1 preferred regimen. [9, 293]

trial_data.n_patients: 519 patients with measurable, previously untreated metastatic, persistent, or recurrent cervical cancer

trial_data.n_patients: 253 patients with metastatic or recurrent cervical cancer

regimens.notes: Carboplatin/paclitaxel is noninferior to cisplatin/paclitaxel and may be used in patients who have received prior cisplatin. [12, 13, 292, 303]

regimens.regimen_name: Pembrolizumab (for TMB-H, PD-L1 positive, or MSI-H/dMMR tumors)
regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 98 patients with advanced cervical cancer previously treated with chemotherapy
trial_data.orr: ORR 12.2% (95% CI, 6.5%–20.4%) overall; 14.6% (95% CI, 7.8%–24.2%) in PD-L1–positive tumors

regimens.notes: Preferred for second-line or subsequent therapy for recurrent/metastatic cervical cancer with disease progression on or after chemotherapy for PD-L1–positive tumors (CPS ≥1). Also preferred for TMB-H and MSI-H/dMMR tumors. [17, 150, 155, 156, 312, 313]

regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 502 patients with recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer
trial_data.os: Median OS 11.5 months vs 9.5 months (HR, 0.70; 95% CI, 0.54–0.89; P = .004)
trial_data.orr: Confirmed ORR 17.8% vs 5.2% (odds ratio, 4.0; 95% CI, 2.1–7.6; P < .0001)

regimens.notes: Preferred therapy option for recurrent/metastatic cervical cancer with disease progression on or after chemotherapy regardless of biomarker status. [18, 19, 317, 319]

trial_data.n_patients: 608 patients with recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer who have progressed on prior therapy
trial_data.pfs: Median PFS 2.8 vs 2.9 months (HR, 0.75; 95% CI, 0.63–0.89; P < .001)

regimens.notes: PD-1–blocking monoclonal antibody with demonstrated OS benefit in recurrent cervical cancer after progression on first-line platinum therapy, regardless of PD-L1 expression. [21, 333, 334]

regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 176 treated, recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer patients; 45 received nivolumab/ipilimumab (NIVO3 plus IPI1)
trial_data.orr: ORR 31% (95% CI, 18–47) with NIVO3 plus IPI1; 40% (95% CI, 26–56) with randomized NIVO1 plus IPI3

regimens.notes: Ipilimumab and nivolumab combination is included based on CheckMate 358 trial data. [22, 23, 24, 335]

regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 19 patients with recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer received nivolumab monotherapy

regimens.notes: Moved from preferred to useful in certain circumstances for PD-L1–positive tumors based on Panel's clinical judgement. [22, 335]

regimens.regimen_name: Pembrolizumab + Tisotumab vedotin-tftv (for PD-L1 positive and no prior IO)
regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 101 patients in dose expansion cohort; 34 received TV + pembrolizumab in second or subsequent setting
trial_data.orr: ORR 35.3% (95% CI, 19.7–53.5) with TV + pembrolizumab in second or subsequent setting

regimens.notes: For patients who are PD-L1–positive and have not received prior immuno-oncology (IO) therapy. [25, 154]

regimens.regimen_name: Fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki (for HER2-positive tumors IHC 3+ or 2+)
regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 267 patients with HER2-expressing locally advanced or metastatic disease; 40 patients with cervical cancer
trial_data.os: In cervical cancer cohort: median OS 13.6 months for HER2 IHC3+
trial_data.orr: Confirmed ORR 50% in cervical cancer; 75% in HER2 IHC3+ cohort

regimens.notes: Antib-drug conjugate for HER2-expressing solid tumors. [26, 168]

regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 18 endocervical adenocarcinoma patients

regimens.notes: Irreversible pan-HER tyrosine kinase inhibitor for HER2-mutant tumors. [27, 169, 170]

regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 41 patients with RET fusion-positive solid tumors (other than non-small cell lung cancer and thyroid cancer)
trial_data.orr: ORR 44% (95% CI, 28.5–60.3); median duration of response 24.5 months (95% CI, 9.2 – NE)

regimens.notes: Tumor-agnostic approval for RET fusion-positive solid tumors. [28, 171, 172]

regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 153 patients with NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors

regimens.notes: TRK inhibitor for NTRK gene fusion-positive tumors. [29, 159, 160, 161]

regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 121 patients with NTRK fusion-positive solid tumors

regimens.notes: TRK inhibitor for NTRK gene fusion-positive tumors. [29, 157, 158, 161, 162]

regimens.setting: Second-line or Subsequent, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 40 patients with advanced solid tumors harboring NTRK fusions (TRK TKI–naive)
trial_data.orr: ORR 58% (95% CI, 41%–73%); median duration of response not reached; 12-month duration of response rate 86%

regimens.notes: TRK inhibitor for NTRK gene fusion-positive tumors, including those who have progressed on prior TRK-targeted therapy. [28, 163]

regimens.regimen_name: Cisplatin/Etoposide (concurrent with chemoradiation and first-line metastatic for NECC)
regimens.setting: First-line, Locally Advanced (Stage IIA-IVA) or Recurrent or Metastatic Disease
regimens.notes: Preferred regimen for chemoradiation and first-line therapy for recurrent or metastatic small cell NECC. Dosing and schedule based on NCCN Guidelines for Small Cell Lung Cancer. [29, 30, 385]

regimens.regimen_name: Carboplatin/Etoposide (concurrent with chemoradiation and first-line metastatic for NECC, cisplatin-intolerant)
regimens.setting: First-line, Locally Advanced (Stage IIA-IVA) or Recurrent or Metastatic Disease
regimens.notes: For patients who are cisplatin intolerant. [29, 30, 385]

regimens.setting: First-line, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: IMpower133: 403 patients with extensive-stage SCLC; CASPIAN: 537 patients with ES-SCLC
trial_data.os: IMpower133: Median OS 12.3 vs 10.3 months (HR, 0.70; 95% CI, 0.54–0.91; P = .007); CASPIAN: Median OS 13.0 vs 10.3 months (HR, 0.73; 95% CI, 0.59–0.91; P = .0047)
trial_data.pfs: IMpower133: Median PFS 5.2 vs 4.3 months (HR, 0.77; 95% CI, 0.62–0.96; P = .02)
trial_data.hr: 0.70 (95% CI, 0.54–0.91) for atezolizumab; 0.73 (95% CI, 0.59–0.91) for durvalumab

regimens.notes: Based on trials in small cell lung cancer, a high-grade neuroendocrine carcinoma. [31, 32, 386, 387]

regimens.setting: First-line, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease

trial_data.n_patients: 13 women treated with TPB for first recurrence vs 21 women treated with non-TPB chemotherapy
trial_data.os: Median OS 9.7 months for TPB vs 9.4 months for non-TPB (HR, 0.53; 95% CI, 0.23–1.22; P = .13)
trial_data.pfs: Median PFS 7.8 months for TPB vs 4.0 months for non-TPB (HR, 0.21; 95% CI, 0.09–0.54; P = .001)

regimens.notes: Topotecan, paclitaxel, and bevacizumab (TPB) significantly improved PFS over non-TPB regimens in recurrent small cell cervical cancer. [33, 389]

regimens.setting: First-line, Recurrent or Metastatic Disease
regimens.notes: Other recommended regimen for first-line therapy for recurrent or metastatic small cell NECC. [29, 30]

trial_data.n_patients: 176 treated, recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer patients; 45 received nivolumab/ipilimumab (NIVO3 plus IPI1)
trial_data.orr: ORR 31% (95% CI, 18–47) with NIVO3 plus IPI1; 40% (95% CI, 26–56) with randomized NIVO1 plus IPI3

regimens.notes: Ipilimumab and nivolumab combination is included based on case reports demonstrating durable response in neuroendocrine cervical carcinoma. [34, 335, 390]
